pr.title: Surface 3D-visible token icons above the vision/fog mask
pr.body:
The screenshot in the issue shows that even when our `testVisibility` override correctly reports a token as visible via height-aware sampling, the token still appears as solid black because Foundry's vision/fog mask is composited on top of the token sprite. The patch was returning `true` but the renderer had already painted darkness over the area behind the wall.

### Changes

- **Hoist the original `testVisibility` reference** out of the patch closure into a module-level `originalTestVisibilityRef` so other code paths can probe the *unpatched* visibility without re-entering our override.
- **Add `isVisibleOnlyVia3D(token)`** which returns true when the original sweep says the token center is *not* visible but at least one height-aware sample point *is* — i.e. the case where the icon would be rendered black.
- **Mirror those tokens into an overlay container** parented to `canvas.interface`, which is rendered above the vision/fog mask. The overlay sprite matches the token's texture, bounds, rotation, and tint.
- **Wire lifecycle hooks** to keep the overlay in sync: `canvasReady`, `canvasTearDown`, `sightRefresh`, `lightingRefresh`, `refreshToken`, `destroyToken`, `deleteToken`. Sprites are removed as soon as the token becomes normally visible, hidden, deleted, or the scene is torn down.

### Scope note

This makes the token *icon* punch through the darkness; the surrounding fog wedge is unchanged. Lighting and explored-area rendering behind the wall would require extending the vision polygon itself and is not addressed here.
pr.author: Copilot
